agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through lifecycle states (trial, active, cancelled)."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect recurring charges. Has a validity/failure status."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when charges are due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the customer's payment method to extend the subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts are made before cancellation (standard tier)."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A 14-day window for Premium tier subscribers during which the subscription remains active despite failed payments, replacing the 3-strike rule."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription that does not charge the customer, limited to one active trial per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives preferential treatment during payment failure (14-day grace period instead of 3-strike cancellation)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method would immediately create an inconsistent state: an active subscription with no ability to collect revenue. This is a data consistency gate, not a policy preference — allowing it would corrupt the billing state from the very first moment.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: any attempt to transition a subscription to 'active' status MUST fail if the associated payment method's status is 'failed'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy tuned for operational efficiency — it gives enough runway for retries. A VP could change this to 5 days or 10 days based on payment processor SLAs or retry strategy without corrupting system state. The system would remain consistent at any reasonable lead time.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: the renewal process initiates exactly 7 days before the current billing cycle's end date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-attempt threshold is a configurable business policy. Management could decide 5 attempts is more customer-friendly, or 2 is more financially prudent. Changing this number does not corrupt state — it simply shifts the cancellation trigger point. It is a policy knob, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when a non-Premium subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts within a single billing cycle, the system transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' status automatically."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. The 'cancelled' state is terminal — it is a one-way door. Allowing reactivation would violate the subscription lifecycle model and could reintroduce subscriptions with stale pricing, expired promotions, or orphaned billing history. If this invariant is violated, the system's state machine is corrupt and downstream processes (invoicing, revenue recognition, analytics) cannot trust subscription state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: no state transition from 'cancelled' to 'active' (or any non-cancelled state) is permitted. Any such attempt MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day duration and the decision to use a grace period instead of strike-counting for Premium are both business policy decisions reflecting a tiered customer retention strategy. The duration could be changed to 21 days, or the policy could be extended to other tiers, without any state corruption. This is a differentiated SLA, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: when a Premium tier subscription has a failed renewal, the system does NOT apply the 3-strike cancellation rule; instead, the subscription remains active for 14 days from the first failure, after which it is cancelled if payment is not successfully collected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness constraint protecting data integrity and preventing abuse. If violated, the system allows a single customer to consume unlimited free resources, directly causing revenue loss and corrupting the business model's fundamental assumption that trials convert to paid subscriptions. Like a unique constraint in a database, this is about the correctness of the data, not a tunable preference.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: at any point in time, for any given email address, the count of subscriptions with status 'active' and type 'free_trial' MUST be <= 1. Any attempt to create a second active trial for the same email MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer attempts to activate a subscription","when":"The payment method on file has a 'failed' status","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be updated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending on date X","when":"The current date reaches X minus 7 days","then":"The system initiates the auto-renewal process and attempts to charge the payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts in the current cycle","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The system automatically transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' status"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any actor (customer, admin, API) attempts to reactivate it","then":"The system rejects the request and instructs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"The first payment failure is recorded","then":"The system starts a 14-day grace period, keeps the subscription active, and does NOT count strikes toward the 3-attempt cancellation rule"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium tier subscription in its grace period","when":"14 days elapse without a successful payment","then":"The system automatically cancels the subscription"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Premium tier subscription in its grace period","when":"A successful payment is collected within the 14-day window","then":"The grace period ends, the subscription continues as active, and the billing cycle resets"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address already associated with an active free trial subscription","when":"A request is made to create a new free trial for the same email","then":"The system rejects the request with an error indicating an active trial already exists"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An email address with a previously completed or cancelled free trial","when":"A request is made to create a new free trial for the same email","then":"The system's behavior depends on business policy (this rule only constrains concurrent active trials, not lifetime trial limits)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between subscription creation and activation","expected_behavior":"If payment method status changes to 'failed' after subscription is created but before activation, the activation must still be blocked per BR-1."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment succeeds on day 13 of grace period","expected_behavior":"Grace period ends, subscription remains active. The system should reset the billing cycle from the successful payment date."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal attempt fails and simultaneously customer updates payment method","expected_behavior":"Race condition: if the 3rd failure is recorded before the payment method update is processed, the subscription is cancelled. The customer must create a new subscription per BR-4."},{"scenario":"Customer downgrades from Premium to Standard tier during a grace period","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the 14-day grace period continue, or does the 3-strike rule immediately apply? Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Free trial subscription is cancelled, then customer immediately requests a new trial","expected_behavior":"Permitted — BR-6 only prohibits concurrent active trials. The cancelled trial is no longer 'active', so a new trial can be created (unless a separate lifetime limit policy exists)."},{"scenario":"Renewal window overlaps with a billing cycle change (e.g., plan upgrade mid-cycle)","expected_behavior":"The 7-day renewal window should reference the current billing cycle's end date at the time renewal is triggered. Mid-cycle changes should recalculate the renewal trigger date."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber has payment failure, then downgrades during grace period, then 3 more failures occur","expected_behavior":"Unclear interaction between tier change and active grace period. Policy decision needed."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Failed payment handling directly impacts revenue recovery. The 7-day retry window and Premium grace period are designed to maximize collection rates. Incorrect implementation of BR-3 or BR-5 could either cancel paying customers prematurely (revenue loss) or let non-paying customers consume resources indefinitely (cost leakage). The free trial uniqueness constraint prevents trial abuse that would erode conversion funnel economics.","operational":"Auto-renewal timing (BR-2) affects payment processor load distribution and retry scheduling. The distinction between 3-strike and grace-period logic for different tiers adds operational complexity to the billing pipeline. Support teams will handle cases where customers dispute automatic cancellations.","user":"The cancelled-is-terminal rule (BR-4) is the most user-facing friction point — customers who lose a subscription with history/config must start fresh. Premium grace periods (BR-5) provide a meaningfully better experience for high-value customers. Trial limits (BR-6) may frustrate users who feel they should get another chance.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions between payment retries and cancellation could strand customers in incorrect states. (2) The Premium grace period vs. 3-strike divergence creates two code paths that must be tested independently. (3) Email-based trial uniqueness can be circumvented with email aliases (user+tag@domain.com) unless normalized.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to strict 3-strike cancellation with no grace period. Must create new subscriptions after cancellation."},{"group":"Customers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Receive preferential 14-day grace period. Higher retention but expectation of continued service during payment issues."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Renewal timing and retry logic directly affect cash flow predictability and revenue recognition timing."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle escalations from cancelled customers who want reactivation. Must enforce BR-4 (no reactivation) consistently."},{"group":"Product/Growth Team","impact":"Free trial limits affect conversion funnel. Trial abuse prevention protects unit economics."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The rules are clearly stated and internally consistent. Confidence is not higher because several interaction patterns between rules are unspecified (tier changes during grace periods, lifetime trial limits vs. concurrent limits, exact retry timing within the 7-day window). These are policy gaps rather than contradictions.","high_confidence_areas":["Subscription activation requires valid payment method (BR-1)","Cancelled state is terminal (BR-4)","Free trial uniqueness per email (BR-6)","Invariant classifications are well-grounded"],"low_confidence_areas":["Behavior when a subscriber changes tier during an active grace period or retry cycle","Whether email normalization is expected for trial uniqueness checks","Exact retry cadence within the 7-day renewal window (daily? exponential backoff?)","Whether the 14-day grace period allows retry attempts or just waits for customer action","Lifetime trial limits vs. concurrent-only trial limits"],"assumptions":["The 3-strike rule applies per billing cycle, not across the subscription lifetime","Premium grace period and 3-strike rule are mutually exclusive — Premium never uses strike counting","Email uniqueness for trials is based on exact email string match unless otherwise specified","Grace period starts from the first failed renewal attempt, not from the billing cycle end date"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through lifecycle states (trial, active, cancelled)."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition"
